meaning: n.(低劣的)新闻笔调,(文笔草率的)新闻文体
examples: His humanity and charm come through in this book despite the author's sometimes grating journalese.
It is a vocabulary that reflects the quality and character about real situation of journalese.
The book’s tone is refreshing, although its occasional passages of lazy journalese can be jarring.
Mr Ledward said many of the claims made about him were simply inaccurate "journalese".
The risk here is a flattening of the voice, a tiredness of imagination, a decline into a sort of poetic journalese the verse equivalent of a clippings job.
